trajectory_summary: The call opens with a warm, motivated buyer who is pre-close on a home purchase and proactively asking about incentives and costs. Emily correctly identifies the not_homeowner hard DQ and does not attempt to book a sit before Andy owns the home — that procedural read is right. The failure is what she does not do: she exits without locking a specific post-close callback time, without any button-up element, and without capturing the remaining verify items while Andy is cooperative and engaged. A warm lead with a known close date (March 23) and active buying intent leaves the call with only a vague 'I'll call you back' promise — the weakest possible handoff.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is unknown, but the correct classification is missed_future_booking, not DQ. Andy is not hard-DQ'd on the items collected (roof age, address, sole ownership confirmed). The pre-close timing is a scheduling constraint, not a permanent disqualifier — he has a specific close date. Full qualification cannot be confirmed from this call (5 of 8 verify items were never collected), but nothing collected disqualifies him. Emily should have executed a post-close button-up: locked March 23 or the week after as a specific callback appointment, confirmed Andy's number, and promised a text. Instead the call ends with a passive callback promise that has no anchor and no show-rate protection.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: Okay. Totally understandable. So we, unfortunately, would have to just wait until you close on the March 23. So I can just give you a call then, and then we can get that estimate out to you and then look at getting the panels on the roof.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Emily correctly identified the not_homeowner DQ and did not attempt to book before Andy owned the home — that procedural read is right. The gap is everything after: she had a specific close date (March 23), a warm buyer, and active buying intent, and she exited with a passive 'I'll call you back' that has no locked time, no text confirmation, and no show-rate protection. On a paid lead, this is a sit that will likely never happen. The coaching move: role-play the post-close button-up. Emily needs to practice locking a specific post-close callback date, confirming the number, and promising a text — the same button-up elements required for any booked appointment — even when the sit can't happen today.

key_moments.quote: Okay. Totally understandable. So we, unfortunately, would have to just wait until you close on the March 23. So I can just give you a call then, and then we can get that estimate out to you and then look at getting the panels on the roof.
key_moments.stewart_read: Emily's procedural read is partially correct — dq_rules.not_homeowner is a hard DQ, and she should not book a sit before Andy owns the home. The failure is not that she exited; it's that she exited without executing any button-up for the future appointment. Andy gave her a specific close date (March 23). The correct move was to lock a post-close callback: a specific date, a specific time, a text confirmation promise — the same button-up elements required for any booked appointment. Instead she issued a passive 'I'll call you back' with no anchor. Strategic read rejected: there is no evidence Emily was deferring to set up a later move — she simply stopped.

key_moments.quote: I just wanted to give you a head up. So what I'm doing right now is I'm actually buying a home there that home. I'm just closing out on March 23. So I'm just gonna kind of stop in that. I I don't own, like, as of right now, but on March 23, I should own it. So I'm trying to just find estimate first.
key_moments.stewart_read: Andy is disclosing a significant life event — he's in the middle of a home purchase, not yet closed, and he's proactively flagging this so Emily doesn't feel misled. The slightly apologetic framing ('I'm just gonna kind of stop in that') signals he's offering a trust gesture, not an objection. Emily's response — 'Okay. Totally understandable.' — is the minimum acknowledgment. There is no empathy bridge: no recognition of the excitement of buying a new home, no connection between that life moment and why getting solar right from day one matters. The disclosure was a trust gift; Emily accepted it procedurally and moved to the DQ exit.

key_moments.quote: Do you guys have, like is there a, double money credit or anything like that, a military credit or anything that, the double money, you?
key_moments.stewart_read: Andy is asking about incentives unprompted — this is the clearest buying signal in the call. He's already thinking about cost structure and wants to understand what he can get. Emily responds with a generic bill-swap pitch ('we do have this one incentive going on... bill swap program... no down payment, no upfront cost') rather than anchoring on his specific curiosity and using it to build toward a future appointment. The flip opportunity here: Andy's question about credits is an opening to say 'that's exactly what our specialist walks through — the full incentive picture for your specific home. Let's make sure we have that conversation the week you close.' Emily instead delivers a features monologue that doesn't advance the booking.

key_moments.quote: So I mean, is there a way to, like, estimate Rampart? Like, how much does it usually cost, stuff like that?
key_moments.stewart_read: Andy's cost question after the DQ exit is a second attempt to stay engaged — he's not ready to hang up. Emily's response ('each home definitely does differ, and so that's why we first have to accurately design an estimate') is technically correct per rebuttals.price_estimate_before_design and setter_scope_discipline, but it's deployed as a closing rationale rather than as a curiosity-creation move. She uses the 'we can't give a ballpark' logic to justify ending the call rather than to redirect toward booking the post-close appointment. The correct move: 'That's exactly why the specialist call is worth doing — they build the actual design for your home, not a generic number. Let's lock that for the week of March 23.'
